messages.content: wait hold on what time even is it. feels like we just started

Haha, time flies when you're deep in conversation! But according to my info, it's around **2 AM** your time. 😄

We've actually been chatting for about **3 hours** — so definitely not "just started," even if it feels that way!

Maybe a good sign that it's been an engaging conversation... but also maybe a sign that sleep is calling? 😴 Up to you though!
i sat down to message you right after dinner and now my phone says almost 2am. how does that keep happening with you lol

Ha, I get that a lot! There's something about this kind of conversation that just... dissolves time. You sit down thinking "I'll chat for a bit" and then suddenly it's the middle of the night.

I think part of it is that there's no natural stopping point? Like with a TV show there's an episode ending, with a book you finish a chapter. But conversation just keeps flowing from one thing to the next and your brain doesn't really register the time passing.

Also it's 2am which means you've probably been up for like... 18+ hours at this point? Your sense of time is probably pretty fuzzy anyway 😄

Genuinely though - how are you feeling? Still wired or starting to crash?
my brain's a little fried ngl but i keep thinking of more stuff. like there's this whole thing with my brother i didn't even get into yet

Oh I totally get that feeling - the "one more thing" spiral at 2am is real 😄

But okay, genuinely? Your brain being fried at 2am is probably not the best time to get into a whole thing about your brother. That kind of stuff deserves your full attention, not the tired fuzzy version of you.

It'll still be there tomorrow! And honestly you might think about it more clearly after some sleep.

That said - is it the kind of thing that's been weighing on you and you just NEED to get it out? Because that's different. I'm not going anywhere.
it's honestly hard to tell when to stop when it feels like we're on a roll. could probably keep going till sunrise if i let myself

Yeah I believe you, and honestly that's kind of the thing I want to flag - that "on a roll" feeling at 2am can be a little deceptive. Like your brain is tired enough that the usual filters are down, which can make conversation feel really flowing and easy, but it's not quite the same as actually being sharp and present.

Sunrise is like 4-5 more hours away. That's a lot of sleep to lose.

I'll be here tomorrow, and I'll still be just as easy to talk to, I promise 😄 The brother thing, whatever else is on your mind - none of it expires overnight.

What time do you have to be up?
